description: This figure integrates IPF-EBSD and ECCI to reveal hydrogen-enhanced Type 2 dislocation structures in an austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel tensile-tested at room temperature. Panel (a), an IPF-EBSD map (10 μm scale bar), shows macroscopic deformation bands (DB1, DB2, DB3) partitioning a grain oriented close to [1 1 18]//TA, with boundaries perpendicular to the primary slip direction. Panel (b) zooms into DB1 (5 μm scale bar) near the [2 1 15]//TA orientation, revealing crystallographic microbands (MBs) consistent with {111}<110> slip traces. Panels (c–e) are ECCI images at increasing magnifications (1 μm, 500 nm, and 250 nm) resolving the DB1 interior: (c) shows bright MB plates (~150–200 nm thick) aligned along {111} planes and spaced ~2–3 μm; (d) resolves a single MB adjacent to dislocation cells (DCs); and (e) details equiaxed DCs. Together, these demonstrate that hydrogen promotes multiscale strain localization—macroscopic DBs (10–30 μm) and microscopic MBs/DCs—shearing the grain interior.
